# mfa_activity

Daily multi-factor authentication (MFA) activity, broken down by factor type and
outcome.

Each row represents the count of MFA attempts for a single combination of day,
MFA factor type, and result.

`Keywords`: MFA, multi-factor, second factor, OTP, FIDO2, authentication, security

## Provenance

Queried directly from the IBM Verify production instance via API.

* **Updated**: Daily, at 6am America/Toronto
* **Steward**: EDCP Data and Research Team
* **Contact**: [Yinlin Zhang](mailto:yinlin.zhang@cds-snc.ca)

## Fields

One row per day per `mfa_type` per `result`.

| Field Name | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| from_date | timestamp | Start of the reporting day (midnight) |
| to_date | timestamp | End of the reporting day (23:59:59) |
| mfa_type | string | The MFA factor used. One of "sms_otp", "fido2", "kq", "totp", "voice_otp", "qr", "ibm_verify_push", "email_otp" |
| result | string | MFA events, One of "success", "failure" or "sent". |
| count | integer | Number of MFA attempts for this day, factor, and result |
| fetched_at | timestamp | When the aggregation last fetched this row |
| date | string | The reporting day as a text date (for example, "2026-05-31"). Mirrors `from_date` |

## Notes

Successfully entering an MFA challenge counts as two events - first, a "sent" event (for
events that are initiated, like email OTP and SMS OTP) and then either a "success" or 
"failure" event, depending on how the user responds. It possible to generate multiple 
"success/failure" events on a single "sent" event if the user inputs their OTP 
incorrectly first and then correctly.

When calculating MFA usage metrics, use the sum of the "sent" events. Sending an email
OTP but not using it is still usage.

When calculating success rate metrics, use the number of successful events divided
by the sum of the success and failure events.
